cabinets.name: Access Approval Cabinet
cabinets.description: Policy-aware access request intake, compliance check, and approval routing for IT and security teams. A routing agent validates every request against role, manager, and SoD policy before handing off a decision-ready approval packet. Keep Okta and Microsoft Entra as your IdP; replace the Google Form inbox, the Slack permission threads, and the untracked manual approvals around them.

cabinets.name: Account Room
cabinets.description: One living workspace per account — stakeholders, history, open opportunities, objections, and next steps in a single place. Keep your CRM. Replace the Notion docs, scattered emails, and Google Docs account briefs around it.

cabinets.name: Ad Performance Cabinet
cabinets.description: Paid media performance in one dashboard — spend, CAC, ROAS, creative winners/losers, and the next experiment queue. Keep your ad platforms and BI — replace the Looker report nobody reads, the weekly Sheets export, and the "what's our CAC this month?" Slack guessing game.

cabinets.name: AE → CSM Handoff
cabinets.description: Turn closed-won deal context into a clean, structured onboarding handoff for Customer Success — stakeholders, promised outcomes, risks, success criteria, and timeline in one packet. Keep Salesforce and your CS tool. Replace the Slack summary, the missed CRM notes, and the 45-minute handoff call.

cabinets.name: Digital Agency
cabinets.description: Digital agency managing multiple client engagements with shared processes and templates.

cabinets.name: AI Hero
cabinets.description: Self-paced AI course — 20 hours of math, intuition, theory, and hands-on LLM construction. Build a neural net from pure Python up through GPT-2, with embedded videos, interactive notebooks, checklists, and a progress dashboard.

cabinets.name: App Directory Cabinet
cabinets.description: Always-current SaaS inventory tracking every app, owner, user count, annual cost, renewal date, and SSO status across your organization. A workflow agent refreshes the inventory weekly; an analyst agent flags underused apps and upcoming renewals before they become surprises. Keep Okta and your finance system; replace the stale spreadsheet nobody trusts.

cabinets.name: Architecture Decision Cabinet
cabinets.description: Maintains a living ADR library — architecture decisions, tradeoffs, owners, diagrams, and rationale — discoverable in one place and kept current. Keep your GitHub and docs. Replace the Confluence page nobody updates and the ADR that lives only in someone's head.

cabinets.name: Audits
cabinets.description: Walk a product, file every friction as a markdown issue, ship fixes with a Senior Product Lead bar — then hand a stakeholder an interactive review slideshow at the end.

cabinets.name: Biology Experiments
cabinets.description: Five interactive simulations of landmark biology experiments — Griffith's transformation, Hershey-Chase, Mendel's peas, Meselson-Stahl, and Miller-Urey. The experiments that proved DNA is the genetic material, that inheritance is particulate, that DNA replication is semiconservative, and that life's building blocks form from a primordial soup. Vanilla JS, runs in the browser.

cabinets.name: Board Finance Cabinet
cabinets.description: Assembles the CFO's board finance section each quarter — ARR, burn multiple, runway, plan vs. actual, cohort retention, and key risks — as a polished, boardroom-ready document sourced entirely from ERP, Stripe, and BI.

cabinets.name: Board Memo Cabinet
cabinets.description: Generate monthly and quarterly board updates covering product, revenue, finance, hiring, risks, and asks. Replaces the manual board pack assembly process — every slide, table, and narrative section is produced from live data files.

cabinets.name: Book Factory
cabinets.description: A complete book-writing OS in a directory. Fill in three placeholders (who you are, what the book is about, format + audience), run the bootstrap prompt, and a book-coach agent builds your premise sheet, a 12-chapter outline, drafts chapter 1, generates cover-art prompts, drafts the back-cover blurb, and recommends a publishing path (KDP / IngramSpark / Draft2Digital / hybrid / query agent route). Designed for non-fiction; adaptable to memoir.

cabinets.name: Brand Cabinet
cabinets.description: Logos, colors, typography, messaging pillars, approved boilerplate copy, and do/don't guidelines — all in one place, always current. Keep Figma and Drive — replace the "which logo file is correct?" Slack thread, the outdated brand PDF nobody can find, and the inconsistent copy that ships because nobody checked.

cabinets.name: Budget Variance Cabinet
cabinets.description: Compares budget vs. actuals by department every month, flags overspend with RAG status, and generates plain-English variance explanations ready for leadership — no more manual pivot tables or emergency Slack threads.

cabinets.name: Bug Escalation Cabinet
cabinets.description: Turns raw customer tickets into engineering-ready bug reports — with reproduction steps, customer impact, ARR at risk, and linked tickets — so engineers get context, not noise. Keep Zendesk, Jira, and Linear. Replace the Slack pings, the half-formed Jira tickets, and the support-to-engineering translation tax.

cabinets.name: Bug Triage Cabinet
cabinets.description: Daily bug triage from Sentry, GitHub, Jira, and Support — bugs ranked by severity, customer impact, and frequency, with owners assigned and priority scores calculated. Keep your issue trackers. Replace the chaotic Sentry inbox and the triage meeting that runs long every time.

cabinets.name: Campaign Launch Cabinet
cabinets.description: End-to-end campaign operations from brief to launch to review. Keep your project tracker and ad platforms — replace the fragmented Notion docs, scattered asset links, and "who's chasing who?" Slack threads.

cabinets.name: Candidate Packet
cabinets.description: Structured candidate packets for hiring committees — resume summary, interview notes digest, take-home assessment, scorecard by competency, and committee recommendation — produced before the debrief. Keeps your ATS as the system of record and replaces Greenhouse notes that nobody reads before the room.

cabinets.name: Career Ops
cabinets.description: AI-powered job search command center. Evaluate offers with a 6-block scoring system, scan 45+ company portals, generate ATS-optimized CVs, and track your pipeline with data-driven precision. Inspired by santifer/career-ops (MIT) by Santiago Fernandez.

cabinets.name: CEO Operating Cabinet
cabinets.description: Run company priorities, leadership decisions, risks, and operating cadence from one place. Replaces the Notion hub, Coda docs, and the dozen leadership trackers nobody updates.

cabinets.name: Change Management Cabinet
cabinets.description: Structured change request documentation, risk scoring, and CAB approval routing for IT and DevOps teams. A QA reviewer agent validates every change packet for completeness and risk before a workflow agent routes it to the right approvers and schedules CAB review. Keep ServiceNow as your ITSM system of record; replace the inconsistent change forms, the forgotten rollback plans, and the CAB scheduling chaos around them.

cabinets.name: Clause Library Cabinet
cabinets.description: A living, searchable library of approved contract clauses — standard language, fallback positions, negotiation notes, and risk explanations — maintained by legal ops and referenced during every contract review. Keep your CLM; replace the "legal playbook" Google Doc buried in a shared drive.

cabinets.name: Company Brain
cabinets.description: The AI-native knowledge base that makes every doc findable and every question answerable. Keep your Drive, SharePoint, and Notion. Replace the chaos of scattered folders, stale pages, and "who has the latest version?" threads.

cabinets.name: Competitive Intelligence
cabinets.description: Cross-company competitive intelligence command center. One Intel Director orchestrates an Analyst and a Signal Scout to watch every competitor — pricing, releases, positioning, funding, hiring, reviews — and publish daily signal scans, weekly competitor briefings, and a monthly landscape review. Every move is captured as an inspectable file.

cabinets.name: Competitive Marketing Cabinet
cabinets.description: The marketing-specific cut of competitive intelligence — messaging, campaigns, SEO footprint, ad presence, and share of voice for every tracked competitor. Keep your CRM and sales tools — replace the Klue/Crayon substitute nobody maintains and the competitor Notion page that's six months stale.

cabinets.name: Compliance Evidence Cabinet
cabinets.description: Collects and maps compliance evidence — policies, screenshots, access logs, vendor docs — to SOC2, ISO 27001, and GDPR controls. Keep Vanta and Drata as your GRC system of record — replace the manual evidence notes, spreadsheet gap trackers, and screenshot folders around them.

cabinets.name: Content Calendar Cabinet
cabinets.description: Plan, draft, schedule, and review all content in one place — from idea to published to performance. Keep your CMS and social tools — replace the Airtable planning doc, the Buffer queue nobody checks, and the "what's going out this week?" thread.

cabinets.name: Content Creator
cabinets.description: Solo content creator operation with strategy, editing, and analytics workflows.

cabinets.name: Contract Intelligence Cabinet
cabinets.description: Turns every executed contract into a structured summary with obligations, renewal terms, risk flags, and owner assignments — all searchable and tracked. Keep DocuSign and Ironclad as your systems of record; replace the contract spreadsheets and the "what did we agree to?" fire drill.

cabinets.name: Contract Renewal Cabinet
cabinets.description: Tracks every contract's renewal date, notice window, auto-renew risk, and pricing change — so legal, finance, and procurement always have a 90-day view of what is expiring and what action is required. Keep your contract repository; replace the renewal spreadsheet and the calendar reminder that fires too late.

cabinets.name: Cooking
cabinets.description: A cabinet for cooking at home — what's in the fridge, what's about to expire, what to make tonight. Recipe Tonight webapp picks dinner from your pantry, weekly meal plan generates the shopping list, recipes are saved with notes so the next time you make them they're better. One agent: Chef.

cabinets.name: Course Factory
cabinets.description: A complete online-course OS in a directory. Fill in three placeholders (who you are, what the course teaches, format + price), run the bootstrap prompt, and a course-creator agent builds your curriculum outline, an 8-module syllabus, drafts the welcome lesson, generates slide prompts, sales-page copy, and a launch-week sequence — plus a platform recommendation (Teachable / Maven / Kajabi / Podia / School / self-hosted).

cabinets.name: Customer Escalation Cabinet
cabinets.description: Converts customer escalation chaos into a structured packet — incident header, timeline, customer impact, root-cause status, owner plan, comms log, and resolution plan — so every escalation is handled with the same rigor, not whoever is loudest in Slack. Three agents replace the ad-hoc Slack threads, manual Jira summaries, and support fire drills.

cabinets.name: Customer Health Cabinet
cabinets.description: Always-on customer health command center. A CSM Lead and Health Analyst track account health scores, usage trends, support tickets, renewal risk, and relationship strength — publishing weekly health refreshes, at-risk alerts, and a live portfolio dashboard. Replaces Gainsight-lite dashboards, Vitally trackers, ChurnZero alerts, and the spreadsheets around them.

cabinets.name: Data Request Cabinet
cabinets.description: Structured intake, triage, and delivery for every business data question — from the Slack "can someone pull this?" to a routed, answered, and filed data request packet. Keep Jira and Slack; replace the untracked data asks that block your team.

cabinets.name: Decision Log Cabinet
cabinets.description: Extracts and preserves every material decision made in meetings, Slack, docs, and email — with owner, rationale, consequences, and status — so the leadership team has a searchable decision register, not archaeology.

cabinets.name: E-commerce Store
cabinets.description: Direct-to-consumer e-commerce brand with inventory, email marketing, and fulfillment operations.

cabinets.name: Employee Offboarding Cabinet
cabinets.description: End-to-end employee offboarding orchestration for IT and HR teams. A workflow agent sequences every step — access revocation, device collection, data transfer, and team notifications — while a compliance agent maintains the audit trail. Keep HRIS, Okta, and Jamf as your systems of record; replace the shared Google Doc checklist that always misses a step.

cabinets.name: Engineering Status Cabinet
cabinets.description: Auto-generated weekly engineering update from GitHub, Jira, and Linear — shipped every Monday before the leadership standup. Keep your issue trackers. Replace the weekly status doc nobody wants to write.

cabinets.name: Experiment Review
cabinets.description: Tracks experiment hypotheses, variants, metrics, statistical results, and decisions in a structured log — producing a readable experiment readout with confidence intervals and a team-accessible experiment library. Keep your Amplitude and Statsig; replace the experiment results Notion doc nobody finishes writing.

cabinets.name: Feature Request
cabinets.description: Ingests feature requests from support tickets, CRM, Slack, and Jira, scores each with a RICE-style framework, and publishes a prioritized request board with revenue impact and segment data. Keep your Jira and CRM; replace the unsorted feature inbox no PM ever has time to triage.

cabinets.name: Finance Memo Cabinet
cabinets.description: Turns raw ERP and payroll data into a polished monthly CFO memo — revenue, burn, runway, expenses, and variance narrative — ready to share with leadership in minutes, not days.

cabinets.name: Fitness
cabinets.description: A cabinet for strength, mobility, and conditioning. The Strength Console tracks your top sets per movement, current cycle, weekly volume, and recovery. Coach plans the week's training, suggests deloads when volume spikes, and keeps the program coherent across cycles. One agent: Coach.

cabinets.name: Help Center Cabinet
cabinets.description: Drafts, updates, and maintains help articles from real customer questions and product release notes — keeping your knowledge base current without manual archaeology. Keep Zendesk Guide or Intercom Articles. Replace the stale docs, the coverage gaps nobody tracks, and the "we should update that article" tickets that never get prioritised.

cabinets.name: Hiring Pipeline
cabinets.description: Talent operations command center — open roles, candidate funnel by stage, time-to-fill, bottlenecks, offer/accept rate, and headcount plan vs. actual. Keeps your ATS as the system of record and replaces the Notion hiring plan and the Sheets tracker that's always three days stale.

cabinets.name: HR Policy Assistant
cabinets.description: Instant, accurate answers to employee policy questions — PTO, benefits, expenses, parental leave, remote work — sourced from your living policy library. Keeps your HRIS and policy docs as the system of record and replaces the Notion/Confluence HR wiki nobody keeps current.

cabinets.name: Incident Postmortem Cabinet
cabinets.description: Builds complete incident timelines, root-cause analyses, and action-item registers from PagerDuty, Datadog, Sentry, and Slack — published within hours of resolution. Keep your alerting stack. Replace the blank Confluence page and the week-late postmortem.

cabinets.name: Internal FAQ
cabinets.description: Lets every employee get instant, sourced answers to HR, IT, finance, and policy questions — without opening a ticket or asking on Slack. Keep your HR and IT systems of record. Replace the Confluence FAQ pages nobody maintains and the Slack threads that answer the same question every week.

cabinets.name: Investor Update Cabinet
cabinets.description: Write monthly investor updates from live company data and leadership notes. Replaces the manual investor email ritual — every update is a sourced, polished artifact that builds investor confidence and keeps your cap table engaged.

cabinets.name: IT Request Cabinet
cabinets.description: Structured IT request intake and routing for modern teams. One routing agent triages every incoming request — hardware, software, access, or support — routes it to the right owner, tracks SLA compliance, and deflects common questions with a living FAQ. Keep ServiceNow and your ticketing system; replace the Slack noise, the Google Form black holes, and the missed SLA fire drills around them.

cabinets.name: Job Hunt HQ
cabinets.description: Job search is a full-time job. This cabinet staffs it with a career strategist, resume tailor, interview coach, and networking scout.

cabinets.name: Keto HQ
cabinets.description: Specialty protocol cabinet for ketogenic eating. Macros, electrolytes, ketone tracking, stall diagnosis, meal planning. Designed to plug into personal-os/health data (labs, weight, workouts) while staying installable standalone. Every recommendation cites a source.

cabinets.name: KPI Narrative Cabinet
cabinets.description: Converts raw dashboard data from Looker, Tableau, and your data warehouse into plain-English business explanations and weekly metric narratives. Keep your BI tools; replace the manual metric docs nobody reads.

cabinets.name: Leadership Meeting Cabinet
cabinets.description: Prepare leadership meeting agendas, summarize decisions, and track action items across every weekly meeting. Replaces the meeting-notes doc nobody reads, the Slack pins that scroll away, and the follow-up tracker that's always a week out of date.

cabinets.name: Legal Request Cabinet
cabinets.description: Structured intake for legal requests from any team — classify type, gather required information, route to the right counsel, and track SLA. Keep your contract and email systems; replace the Slack legal thread, the Google Form, and the work-in-progress Jira queue nobody audits.

cabinets.name: Manager 1:1
cabinets.description: Per-report 1:1 workspace for managers — recurring agenda, running notes timeline, open action items, goals tracker, and feedback log — so every 1:1 is prepared and nothing important gets lost in scattered Docs. Keeps your HRIS and Calendar as the system of record and replaces the Notion pages nobody updates between sessions.

cabinets.name: Meeting Memory
cabinets.description: Captures every meeting as structured memory — summaries, decisions, action items, and owners — so nothing falls through the cracks. Keep your calendar and conferencing tools. Replace the scattered notes, forgotten follow-ups, and "what did we decide?" threads.

cabinets.name: Metrics Definition Cabinet
cabinets.description: The canonical glossary for every business metric — owner, formula, source table, and certified status. Keep dbt and your warehouse; replace the Notion metric doc nobody trusts and the Slack thread that contradicts it.

cabinets.name: Mom Command
cabinets.description: The always-installed root for the Mom & Baby cabinet series. Holds the shared family context — baby profile, growth chart, feed/sleep/diaper logs, milestone log, vaccination schedule, pediatrician info, emergency contacts. Pairs with one stage cabinet at a time (newborn, infant, crawler, etc.). One agent: Martha, your chief of staff.

cabinets.name: Music Factory
cabinets.description: A browser-native MIDI factory. Pick a famous public-domain song, watch the piano roll, listen via Web Audio synthesis, download a real .mid file. Ships with Ode to Joy, Für Elise, Twinkle Twinkle, Jingle Bells, Happy Birthday, Tetris (Korobeiniki), Canon in D, and When the Saints Go Marching In — plus the original Portal "Still Alive" player as a sub-app.

cabinets.name: New Hire Onboarding
cabinets.description: Structured onboarding workspace for every new employee — pre-boarding to 30-day mark. Keeps your HRIS as the system of record and replaces the Notion pages, BambooHR checklists, and scattered Slack DMs that make onboarding chaotic.

cabinets.name: Newborn (0–3 months)
cabinets.description: The survival cabinet for weeks 0–12 with a brand-new baby. Pairs with mom-command (the always-installed root). One agent: Martha, your newborn counselor — same Martha as every other Mom & Baby cabinet, just titled for this stage. Full-screen Survival Tracker webapp, 20 short wiki pages on newborn essentials, milestone CSV, and a clear red-flags page for when to call the doctor.

cabinets.name: Newsletter Factory
cabinets.description: A complete newsletter OS in a directory. Fill in three placeholders (who you are, what the newsletter is about, your style and cadence), run the bootstrap prompt, and an editor-in-chief agent builds your brand voice, a 12-issue content calendar, drafts the launch issue, generates subject-line variants, and recommends a platform (Substack / Beehiiv / ConvertKit / Ghost) with growth-loop tactics.

cabinets.name: Office Ops Cabinet
cabinets.description: Keeps the physical office running — visitor management, supply levels and reorders, facilities tickets, recurring maintenance tasks, and office request triage — all in one place. Keep your calendar and Slack. Replace the Envoy-lite spreadsheet, the Robin-lite room tracker tab, and the facilities requests that vanish into the ether.

cabinets.name: OKR Command Cabinet
cabinets.description: The living OKR board for your company and every department. Tracks objectives, key results, owners, confidence, and blockers — then ships a weekly narrative so leadership never has to ask "how are we tracking?"

cabinets.name: Performance Review
cabinets.description: Structured performance review packets generated from goals, manager notes, peer feedback, and shipped work — one packet per employee per review cycle. Keeps your HRIS as the system of record and replaces the Lattice/15Five templates nobody fills in thoughtfully.

cabinets.name: Personal OS
cabinets.description: Your life has departments. Staff them. A second-brain / personal-assistant cabinet with six life areas — brain, family, home, money, health, play — each with its own agents, jobs, and working files.

cabinets.name: Physics 101
cabinets.description: A 6-module beginner physics curriculum — Motion, Forces, Energy, Waves, Electricity, Light. Each module follows the same pedagogy: hook, core concepts, interactive element, worked example, practice questions, bridge to the next module. No calculus assumed; algebra and basic geometry only.

cabinets.name: Physics Experiments
cabinets.description: Five interactive, self-contained simulations of classic physics experiments — double-slit, pendulum wave, projectile motion, wave interference, and the brachistochrone race. Vanilla JS + Canvas 2D, zero build step, runs entirely in the browser. Each experiment is a full-screen Cabinet app you can open from the sidebar.

cabinets.name: Pipeline Risk
cabinets.description: Identify risky deals, stale opportunities, missing champions, and weak next steps before they cost you the quarter. Keep Salesforce. Replace the manual deal-review docs and the Clari spreadsheet nobody trusts.

cabinets.name: Podcast Factory
cabinets.description: A complete Podcast OS in a directory. Fill in three placeholders (who you are, what the show is about, your style), run the bootstrap prompt, and a podcast-producer agent builds out your brand page, a 12-episode content calendar, scripts, shownotes, cover-art prompts, and a recording-platform decision guide (Riverside, StreamYard, Zencastr, audio-only).

cabinets.name: PRD Builder
cabinets.description: Generates structured PRD drafts from customer pain, business goals, user stories, and constraints — then runs a completeness QA pass to ensure every section is production-ready before handoff to engineering. Keep your Jira; replace the half-empty Google Doc that engineers pad with assumptions.

cabinets.name: Procurement Intake
cabinets.description: Turns every vendor and tool request into a structured approval packet — cost, risk, alternatives, compliance flags, and a decision-ready owner chain — before it touches a budget holder. Keep your ERP and finance system. Replace the Google Form, the Airtable tracker, and the Slack thread nobody follows up on.

cabinets.name: Product Launch
cabinets.description: Manages the full product launch lifecycle — checklist by workstream, owners, asset tracker, risk log, internal comms, and release notes — with a live countdown and daily chase near launch. Keep your Jira; replace the launch spreadsheet with 8 owners and no one driving.

cabinets.name: Proposal / RFP
cabinets.description: Draft proposals and RFP responses using customer context, approved pricing, security questionnaire answers, and case studies — with a compliance check and QA pass before it leaves the room. Keep your CRM and legal docs. Replace the Loopio spreadsheets, the last-minute Drive scrambles, and the 3-day RFP fire drill.

cabinets.name: QBR Generator Cabinet
cabinets.description: Turns raw customer data into polished Quarterly Business Review content — exec summary, goals vs outcomes, usage and adoption, ROI realized, open issues, and next-quarter plan. A Writer and Analyst team replaces the Google Slides QBR grind, Gainsight template exports, and manual deck assembly. Every QBR is a structured file you can review, edit, and present.

cabinets.name: Reading Room
cabinets.description: A cabinet for what you read — TBR, currently reading, finished, with star ratings, three-bullet "what I learned", and a year-in-review wall. Goodreads but private, beautiful, and your data on your disk. Librarian curates.

cabinets.name: Real Estate Brokerage
cabinets.description: Real estate brokerage with listings management, marketing, and client relationship operations.

cabinets.name: Release Notes Cabinet
cabinets.description: Turns merged PRs and completed issues into polished internal and customer-facing release notes on every release cycle. Keep your GitHub and Jira. Replace the manual changelog, the rushed release email, and the "someone write the release notes" ask.

cabinets.name: Renewal Risk Cabinet
cabinets.description: Structured renewal management command center that surfaces upcoming renewals, risk levels, expansion potential, and required actions across 90/60/30-day windows. A CSM agent and a Workflow agent replace the manual renewal tracker spreadsheets, Gainsight notes, and quarterly renewal fire drills. Every renewal has an action plan on disk.

cabinets.name: Risk Register Cabinet
cabinets.description: Tracks company security and operational risks with owners, likelihood-impact scores, mitigation plans, and review cadence. Keep your GRC platform as the system of record — replace the Notion risk pages, unreviewed spreadsheets, and ad-hoc risk conversations around it.

cabinets.name: Roadmap
cabinets.description: Builds data-backed roadmap proposals from goals, customer feedback, capacity, and business impact — producing a structured Now/Next/Later plan with confidence ratings and linked evidence. Keep your Jira and Linear; replace the roadmap deck that's stale before it's presented.

cabinets.name: SaaS Startup
cabinets.description: B2B SaaS startup with product-led growth, engineering, and customer success teams.

cabinets.name: Sales Battlecard
cabinets.description: Per-competitor battlecards with objection handling, proof points, pricing deltas, and recommended collateral — always current, agent-refreshed. Keep your CRM and Gong. Replace the stale Highspot decks and the PMM Confluence page nobody updates.

cabinets.name: Sales Call Prep
cabinets.description: Prep reps for every call with account context, recent activity, likely pain points, discovery questions, and competitive notes — delivered before 7 AM for that day's calendar. Keep your CRM and Gong. Replace the 20-minute pre-call tab scramble.

cabinets.name: Security Questionnaire Cabinet
cabinets.description: Auto-answers customer and vendor security questionnaires using your existing policies, SOC2 docs, and past answers. Keep Vanta and Drata as your evidence systems of record — replace the questionnaire spreadsheets, copy-paste marathons, and stale answer libraries around them.

cabinets.name: SEO Content Cabinet
cabinets.description: Keyword research, content briefs, rankings tracking, and refresh tasks — all in one workspace. Keep Search Console and your CMS — replace the Ahrefs spreadsheet, the brief-in-Docs nobody updates, and the "which pages need refreshing?" question nobody can answer.

cabinets.name: Spend Policy Cabinet
cabinets.description: Answers "can I expense this?" instantly, flags anomalous transactions before month-end, and keeps department spend summaries current — so Finance spends less time on policy Slack questions and more time on analysis.

cabinets.name: Sprint Planning Cabinet
cabinets.description: Automated sprint preparation and daily standup digest for engineering teams. Keep your Jira and Linear. Replace the sprint-planning docs, the copy-paste capacity math, and the Monday "what's blocking you?" ritual.

cabinets.name: Strategic Initiative Cabinet
cabinets.description: The initiative room for cross-functional strategic programs — pricing changes, market launches, reorgs. Tracks milestones, owners, risks, and blockers so strategy leads and PMO have one view instead of five.

cabinets.name: Support Intelligence Cabinet
cabinets.description: Clusters support tickets by theme, surfaces recurring pain patterns, and ships a weekly insights report — so your support team stops firefighting noise and starts fixing root causes. Keep Zendesk or Intercom. Replace the manual tagging, the ad-hoc reports, and the "why do customers keep asking about X?" Slack thread.

cabinets.name: Support Macro Cabinet
cabinets.description: Generates, QA-reviews, and maintains a library of reusable support macros from real ticket clusters — so every rep pulls a reviewed, on-brand reply instead of writing from scratch. Keep Zendesk macros or Intercom snippets. Replace the outdated canned responses, the Google Doc nobody updates, and the tone drift nobody catches.

cabinets.name: Team Wiki
cabinets.description: A living team page with responsibilities, active projects, rituals, key docs, and on-call ownership — always current, always findable. Keep your project tools and Slack. Replace the stale Notion team page nobody updates and the "who owns X?" DMs.

cabinets.name: Text Your Mom
cabinets.description: Relatable B2C app company cabinet used to test nested cabinet behavior.

cabinets.name: Universal Request Cabinet
cabinets.description: One intake flow for every team — marketing, IT, finance, design, legal, data, and ops. Requests are categorised, routed to the right owner, assigned an SLA, and tracked to completion. Keep your project tools. Replace the five Google Forms, the Typeform nobody checks, and the Jira queue that became a black hole.

cabinets.name: USA Travel Planner
cabinets.description: An inspiration board for exploring the United States — interactive map of all 63 national parks with been-there checklist, every state fair, federal holidays, iconic regional fast-food chains, and a live event-hunter agent that scans X and news for cool things happening across the country.

cabinets.name: Vendor / Asset Registry
cabinets.description: The single source of truth for every piece of equipment, software licence, and vendor relationship the company owns — with assigned owners, renewal dates, costs, and compliance status maintained automatically. Keep your finance system and IdP. Replace the Airtable spreadsheet nobody trusts and the asset-tracking tab that's always six months out of date.

cabinets.name: Vendor Renewal Cabinet
cabinets.description: Tracks every SaaS vendor renewal date, notice window, owner, and spend in one place — sends alerts before cancellation windows close and surfaces spend concentration so Finance and Procurement can negotiate from strength.

cabinets.name: Vendor Security Review Cabinet
cabinets.description: Assesses vendor risk through SOC2 reports, DPA status, sub-processor lists, and security questionnaires — producing a structured approval packet for every new and renewing vendor. Keep Vanta and Drata as your GRC system of record — replace the security review spreadsheets, ad-hoc Slack requests, and manual approval emails around them.

cabinets.name: VC Cabinet
cabinets.description: Run an early-stage venture firm from one knowledge base. Five agents source deal flow and score it against your thesis, monitor portfolio health and flag runway risk before it's a crisis, research markets and companies on demand, and publish a daily point of view on your vertical to compound proprietary deal flow. Replaces the scattered stack of CRMs, spreadsheets, and "I'll get to it" follow-ups that every fund secretly runs on.

cabinets.name: Voice of Customer
cabinets.description: Collects, clusters, and quantifies customer feedback across every channel — support, sales calls, reviews, and chat — turning raw signals into ranked themes with revenue impact. Keep your CRM and Zendesk; replace the feedback spreadsheet nobody trusts.

cabinets.name: Wedding Planner
cabinets.description: A complete wedding-planning OS in a directory. Fill in three placeholders (who you are, your vision, your budget + date), run the bootstrap prompt, and a planner agent builds your vendor matrix, budget tracker, 18-month timeline, guest-list scaffold, vow + speech generators, RSVP system, and a day-of runbook. One agent, two routines, complete sanity in the chaos.

cabinets.name: Weekly Business Review Cabinet
cabinets.description: Auto-generates the weekly business review across revenue, product, support, engineering, and finance — so leadership walks into Monday with a sourced, cross-functional memo, not a collection of disconnected slides.

cabinets.name: YouTube Channel Factory
cabinets.description: A complete YouTube OS in a directory. Fill in three placeholders (who you are, what your channel is about, your style and format), run the bootstrap prompt, and a creative-director agent builds out your channel brand, a 12-video content calendar, scripts, thumbnail prompts, and a production-gear decision guide (DSLR vs mirrorless vs phone, editing software, audio chain).
